title: enum(...) — named closed sets
manager: A named closed set of symbolic values — `enum(red, green, blue)` — for the countless machine variables that are "one of a short list," modeled as a real type instead of magic strings. The checker knows the full set, so an exhaustive `case` over an enum can be proven complete, and an impossible value becomes a compile error rather than a runtime typo that surfaces in production as a state nobody handled.
cs: closed symbolic set; exhaustiveness-checkable in `case` (W6.41); numeric-backed enums behave per the val numeric-enum fix (Tjssm-759); intensional membership.
